label: OEM data window start
sourceNote: Exact epoch from committed normalized official NASA Artemis II OEM dataset (data/normalized/artemis-2.json).
description: First available sample in the committed Artemis II normalized OEM file.

label: OEM data window end
sourceNote: Exact epoch from committed normalized official NASA Artemis II OEM dataset (data/normalized/artemis-2.json).
description: Last available sample in the committed Artemis II normalized OEM file; this committed OEM endpoint is before splashdown.

label: Modeled terminal descent extension begins
sourceNote: Derived directly from the final committed official OEM sample to bridge the gap to splashdown.
description: Transition point where modeled terminal descent starts beyond the current official OEM endpoint.

label: Entry interface (modeled timing)
sourceNote: Estimated from Orion nominal entry/parachute sequence; replace with exact Artemis II timestamps when verified.
description: Atmospheric entry begins at the current committed OEM endpoint in this visualization.

label: Drogue parachutes deploy (estimated)
sourceNote: Estimated from Orion nominal entry/parachute sequence; replace with exact Artemis II timestamps when verified.
description: Two drogue chutes deploy during terminal descent (modeled timing).

label: Main parachutes deploy (estimated)
sourceNote: Estimated from Orion nominal entry/parachute sequence; replace with exact Artemis II timestamps when verified.
description: Three main chutes deploy for final deceleration (modeled timing).

label: Pacific splashdown off San Diego
sourceNote: Splashdown UTC and regional location are mission facts from NASA Artemis II public mission material.
description: Orion splashdown in the eastern Pacific off San Diego, California.

surfaceTarget.label: Pacific splashdown area off San Diego
